title: Software Development Speed: 100 MPH vs 4 MPH? The Great Divide #shorts
summary: A short-form video examines the stark differences in software development speed between companies. Some organizations ship features rapidly while others move slowly, highlighting a key divide in tech innovation and engineering productivity.
writeup: This short from Cyril Imhof contrasts the extreme range in software development speed across tech companies, from rapid feature launches to slow iteration cycles. It frames the gap as a structural divide in engineering productivity and innovation capability rather than a matter of individual effort. The takeaway: development velocity and engineering workflows vary widely enough across the industry to separate firms that ship quickly from those that iterate slowly.
